question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NullPointerException by ensuring the pod named by HOSTNAME does not exist in Kubernetes, causing client.pods().withName(hostname).get() to return null, so that .getMetadata().getLabels() throws NPE, which is silently swallowed by onErrorComplete() and results in a permanent UNKNOWN health status masking real system state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that System.getenv('HOSTNAME') is used without sanitization as a direct Kubernetes pod name lookup, so that if the HOSTNAME environment variable is set to a crafted value (e.g., containing path traversal or special characters), the Kubernetes API call client.pods().withName(hostname).get() is misdirected, causing incorrect label resolution and wrong HelmRelease name retrieval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by repeatedly triggering health() checks before the cache is populated, flooding the Kubernetes API with client.pods().withName(hostname).get() calls, exhausting Kubernetes API server rate limits and degrading cluster-wide API availability?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user cause Objects.requireNonNull(labels.get(INSTANCE_LABEL), ...) to throw a NullPointerException by ensuring the pod's metadata labels map does not contain the 'app.kubernetes.io/instance' key, which is then silently swallowed by onErrorComplete(), permanently caching an UNKNOWN health state and hiding real release status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmRelease()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of null-check on client.pods().withName(hostname).get().getMetadata() — where getMetadata() itself could return null — to trigger a NullPointerException that is silently consumed by onErrorComplete(), causing the health indicator to permanently return UNKNOWN and masking real infrastructure failures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .cacheInvalidateIf(v -> false) predicate — which never invalidates the cached HelmRelease name — so that a stale pod label value is permanently used for all subsequent Kubernetes HelmRelease lookups, causing the health indicator to report incorrect status indefinitely after a pod label change?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .onErrorComplete() call on the getHelmRelease() step to silently suppress any Kubernetes API authentication or authorization error, causing the health indicator to fall through to UNKNOWN status and hide a real security misconfiguration (e.g., revoked service account token) from operators?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the second .onErrorComplete() call on the getHelmReleaseReadyStatus() step to silently suppress ClassCastException from unchecked casts, causing the health indicator to return UNKNOWN instead of DOWN, misleading operators into believing the system is in an indeterminate rather than failed state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a thread leak by triggering repeated bean re-initialization scenarios where Schedulers.newSingle('helmrelease-health-cache') is called each time createHelmReleaseHealth() is invoked, creating unbounded new single-threaded schedulers that are never disposed, leading to resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: createHelmReleaseHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .switchIfEmpty(UNKNOWN) fallback — triggered when both onErrorComplete() calls suppress errors — to cause the health endpoint to always report UNKNOWN during a real outage, preventing automated alerting systems from detecting a DOWN state and delaying incident response?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NullPointerException by ensuring the HelmRelease Kubernetes resource named by the pod label exists but has no 'status' field in its additional properties, causing (Map<String, Object>) resource.getAdditionalProperties().get('status') to return null, and the subsequent status.get('conditions') call to throw NPE?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a ClassCastException by ensuring the HelmRelease resource's 'status' field is not a Map<String, Object> (e.g., it is a String or List), causing the unchecked cast (Map<String, Object>) resource.getAdditionalProperties().get('status') to throw ClassCastException, which is silently swallowed by onErrorComplete()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a ClassCastException by ensuring the HelmRelease resource's 'conditions' field is not a List<Map<String, String>> (e.g., it is a Map or primitive), causing the unchecked cast (List<Map<String, String>>) status.get('conditions') to throw ClassCastException, silently swallowed by onErrorComplete(), resulting in UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NullPointerException by ensuring the HelmRelease resource exists but its 'status' map contains a null value for 'conditions', causing status.get('conditions') to return null and the subsequent conditions.stream() call to throw NPE, silently swallowed by onErrorComplete()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NullPointerException by ensuring the HelmRelease Kubernetes resource lookup client.genericKubernetesResources(RESOURCE_DEFINITION_CONTEXT).withName(release).get() returns null (resource not found), causing resource.getAdditionalProperties() to throw NPE, silently swallowed by onErrorComplete()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on the 'release' string (derived from pod label 'app.kubernetes.io/instance') to perform a Kubernetes API enumeration attack, where a crafted label value causes the client to query arbitrary HelmRelease resource names in the namespace, leaking existence information about other releases?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHelmReleaseReadyStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException by ensuring an individual condition map entry within the 'conditions' list is null, so that condition.get('type') in the filter lambda throws NPE, which is silently swallowed by onErrorComplete(), resulting in UNKNOWN health status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the health indicator to permanently return DOWN by injecting a HelmRelease condition where the 'status' field is set to a value other than 'True' and the 'reason' field is set to a value other than DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY, causing mapStatus() to always return DOWN even when the release is transitionally upgrading, triggering false-positive alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mapStatus() only checks condition.get('status').equals('True') and condition.get('reason').equals(DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY) without null-checking either value, so that a null 'status' or 'reason' field in the condition map causes a NullPointerException, silently swallowed by onErrorComplete()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: mapStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY string comparison in mapStatus() to force the health indicator to return UNKNOWN (instead of DOWN) by crafting a HelmRelease condition with reason='DependencyNotReady', masking a real failure state and preventing automated remediation from triggering?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when properties.isEnabled() is false (the default), health() always returns the static UP Mono regardless of actual Kubernetes HelmRelease state, causing the health endpoint to report UP even when the release is genuinely DOWN, hiding real failures from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnNext(this::recordHealthMetric) side effect in health() to cause the static RELEASE_UP AtomicInteger to be set to 0 (DOWN) by triggering an error path that results in UNKNOWN status, causing Micrometer gauge metric 'hiero.mirror.monitor.health' to report 0 and trigger false-positive alerts in monitoring dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a race condition by concurrently invoking the health() endpoint many times before the reactive cache is populated, causing multiple simultaneous Kubernetes API calls for getHelmRelease() and getHelmReleaseReadyStatus(), exhausting connection pool resources or triggering Kubernetes API rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static AtomicInteger RELEASE_UP shared across all instances of ReleaseHealthIndicator in the same JVM to cause metric corruption, where one instance's health result overwrites another's, leading to incorrect Micrometer gauge readings and false alerting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ReleaseHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: '] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where recordHealthMetric() receives a null Health object (currentHealth == null), causing the null-check to fall through to Status.UNKNOWN, setting RELEASE_UP to 0, and causing the Micrometer gauge to report DOWN when the system is actually UP?
